title: Japan's audacious sample-return mission to the Mars moon Phobos has made it to the launch pad
summary: Japan's MMX mission has reached the launch pad, aiming to return samples from Mars' moon Phobos by 2031 to study the origins of Phobos and Deimos.

title: Our recommended choice as the best beginner mirrorless camera has $400 off, and has just hit its best price of the year at Best Buy
summary: The Sony A7C II mirrorless camera has hit its best price of the year at Best Buy with a $400 discount. It was praised by expert tester Kimberley Lane for its impressive astrophotography capabilities and affordability.

title: Making contact with ET? Aliens may already know we're here
summary: Aliens may already be aware of Earth's existence due to human-made structures that could signal intelligent life. These massive constructions could reveal our presence and technological advancements to extraterrestrial observers.

title: 'That's just not a winning strategy': Congress objects (again) to Trump's planned NASA budget cuts
summary: Congress opposes President Trump's proposed NASA budget cuts during a House hearing, with bipartisan objections raised. Members from both sides of the aisle criticized the plan as ineffective.

title: From 'Starfield' to Artemis: designing moonbases in games and reality
summary: The article compares lunar settlement designs in video games like 'Starfield' with NASA's real-life plans for Artemis Base Camp. It explores how gaming concepts align with or differ from actual space mission architectures.

title: Rocket Lab launches Japanese 'origami' satellite, 7 other spacecraft to orbit (photos)
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched eight Japanese satellites into orbit, including one equipped with an origami-folded antenna. The mission took place on April 22.

title: NASA still confident that Artemis astronauts will land on the moon in 2028 despite spacesuit delays
summary: NASA remains confident that Artemis astronauts will land on the moon in 2028 despite potential spacesuit delays. A new report suggests the suits might face a multiyear delay.

title: Happy Earth Day! Celebrate with these amazing photos of our planet from space, from Apollo 8 to Artemis 2
summary: Earth Day is celebrated with historical photos of Earth from space, spanning missions from Apollo 8 to Artemis 2.

title: Meet the US Navy divers who welcomed the Artemis 2 astronauts home from the moon
summary: Four US Navy divers played a key role in the Artemis 2 mission by retrieving the astronauts upon their return to Earth and ensuring their safety. Their efforts contributed to the success of the historic lunar mission.

title: Smoke Shrouds Northern Thailand
summary: Seasonal fires in Northern Thailand have caused smoke to shroud the region, significantly darkening skies over Southeast Asia.

title: NASA SBIR/STTR Phase I and II BAA, 2026 Appendix A and B are now live! Offers due May 21, 2026, 5:00PM EDT
summary: NASA has opened its SBIR/STTR Phase I and II BAA for 2026, with offers due by May 21, 2026. The program aims to fund innovative research and development projects.

title: NASA’s 777 Aircraft Returns Home with Science Flights on the Horizon
summary: NASA's modified Boeing 777 has returned to its fleet after structural modifications to serve as an airborne science laboratory. The aircraft arrived at Langley Research Center in Virginia after a check flight and transit from Waco.

title: NASA Targets Early September for Roman Space Telescope Launch
summary: NASA has set an early September 2026 launch target for the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, ahead of its commitment to launch no later than May 2027. The accelerated development is attributed to collaboration between public investment, institutional expertise, and private enterprise.

title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon to Discuss Upcoming Launch, Mission
summary: NASA will hold a news conference on April 29 to preview astronaut Anil Menon's upcoming mission to the International Space Station. The event will be livestreamed on NASA's YouTube channel.

title: Night and (Earth) Day
summary: NASA released an image of Earth's terminator line, captured by the Artemis II astronauts during their lunar journey on April 2, 2026, in celebration of Earth Day. The agency highlighted its ongoing contributions to Earth science and improving life on our home planet.

title: Advancing Earth Observation at NASA Since Release of Earthrise Photo
summary: NASA's Earth observation technology has advanced significantly since the iconic Earthrise photo in 1968, evolving from simple spacecraft window cameras to powerful radar systems. The agency's mission to understand Earth has remained constant despite these technological improvements.

title: Artemis II Mission Milestones: An Image and Video Recap
summary: The Artemis II mission launched on April 1, 2026, with a crew of four astronauts on a nearly 10-day journey around the Moon, marking the first crewed flight of NASA's Orion spacecraft. The mission concluded on April 10 with a successful splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off San Diego.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4867-4872: Sand Fill In Antofagasta Crater and Finding Our Next Drill Target
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover reached the rim of the Antofagasta crater on Mars, a 10-meter-wide feature that appears fresh and deep. The rover is now preparing to identify its next drilling target.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the timeline and progress of the spacesuit development.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven space missions, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches, are scheduled across four countries using five different launch vehicles during the week of April 20.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after the launch aboard Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This follows Blue Origin's recent milestone of landing a previously flown first-stage booster.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an astronaut flight suit and a large docking adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality. This innovation supports the development of commercial space stations.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fires as part of the Artemis program. This milestone follows the successful return of Orion.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew on the International Space Station conducted vehicle departures and a spacewalk in March. The activities were highlighted in a report by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will conduct its third launch using a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool mission concluding its CRS NG-23 resupply flight.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marked a significant milestone for NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned, but no specific details about space industry news are provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores key milestones in the agency's history and contributions to space exploration.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and space debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has passed rigorous space-like tests, confirming it is on track for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on June 8-9 in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April-May. The event aims to gather feedback on topics affecting the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement was strategically chosen for optimal performance.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved guidance or navigation systems. It highlights a specific innovation or achievement in this area.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team conducted experiments with graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025, demonstrating that lasers can propel these ultralight materials in microgravity conditions. The study, led by Université Libre de Bruxelles and Khalifa University, suggests potential applications for steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions in space.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center. The engine could potentially power future Moon missions.

title: UNIVITY Raises €27M Series A
summary: UNIVITY has raised €27 million in a Series A funding round led by Blast Club and Expansion Venture Capital, with additional support from Bpifrance's Deep Tech fund.

title: Lawmakers Promise to Reject Proposed NASA Cuts—Again
summary: Lawmakers have vowed to reject proposed budget cuts to NASA, stating the proposal cannot support the agency's mandated goals. The statement comes in response to President Trump's directives for NASA.

title: Tracking Sats From the Sea
summary: The article discusses how satellites provide domain awareness to protect Navy ships and suggests that the Navy could assist in tracking these satellites. It highlights a potential mutual benefit between satellite operations and naval activities.

title: ATMOS Space Cargo Raises €25.7M Series A
summary: ATMOS Space Cargo has raised €25.7 million in a Series A funding round. The company plans to use the funds to advance its operational services and develop its ATMOS WORKS and PHOENIX 3 architectures for commercial, institutional, and defense applications.

title: Payload Field Guide: Golden Dome
summary: Golden Dome, initially announced in January 2025, is a missile defense program aimed at protecting the US. Companies are actively positioning themselves to compete for this ambitious initiative.

title: Opportunities Beyond the Moon Opened by CLPS
summary: NASA plans to launch monthly uncrewed missions to the Moon starting next year through its CLPS program, creating opportunities for various companies beyond those focused solely on lunar activities.

title: Economics of Orbital Data Centers Report: Part 1
summary: The first part of a four-part report analyzes the economic factors driving the push for orbital data centers. It highlights terrestrial bottlenecks as a key reason for moving compute resources to space.

title: NASA’s 2028 Moon Landing Has A Spacesuit Problem, New Report Says
summary: NASA's planned 2028 Moon landing may be delayed due to delays in spacesuit development, according to a new report from the Office of the Inspector General. The report highlights challenges in the development of the next-generation spacesuits needed for the mission.

title: Latvia To Join Artemis Accords Today
summary: Latvia is set to join the Artemis Accords today, marking another international partner in the program. Mike Gold of Redwire emphasized the need for countries to move beyond signing the accords to actively contributing to the Artemis program.

title: US Officials See Proposed European Union Space Act as Complicated Overreach
summary: The European Union is drafting the EU Space Act, which has raised concerns among U.S. officials about potential overreach. The proposed act aims to create a unified regulatory framework for space activities within the EU.

title: Australian Airline Jetstar Selects Viasat for IFC for Dreamliner Fleet
summary: Australian low-cost airline Jetstar Airways has selected Viasat's Amara solution for in-flight connectivity on its Boeing 787 Dreamliner fleet. The Amara system will be installed across 11 Jetstar aircraft.

title: SSC Space and Kuva Space to Collaborate in Nordic Region
summary: SSC Space and Kuva Space have announced a collaboration to enhance space-based capabilities in the Nordic region across the space value chain. The partnership aims to strengthen regional space infrastructure and services.

title: FCC Grants AST SpaceMobile Commercial Authorization for Direct-to-Device Service
summary: The FCC has granted AST SpaceMobile commercial authorization to provide direct-to-device satellite services using spectrum from partner mobile network operators. This approval marks a significant step for the company's efforts to expand mobile broadband connectivity globally.

title: Eutelsat Signs New Broadcast Deals in Mexico and Caribbean
summary: Eutelsat has signed three new broadcast deals in Mexico and the Caribbean, partnering with Co-op Cable to expand direct-to-home (DTH) and connectivity services.

title: Pentagon to Move Forward On AEP Upgrades for GPS, As DoD Cancels GPS OCX
summary: The Pentagon has decided to cancel the GPS Next Generation Operational Control System (OCX) by RTX and instead proceed with upgrades to the existing ground system for GPS. The move involves advancing the Lockheed Martin Architecture Evolution Plan (AEP) upgrades.

title: Intellian Details New Terminals for Aviation and WGS Network
summary: Intellian Technologies announced two new terminals, including its first aviation terminal and a new tactical flyaway terminal, expanding its satellite technology offerings.

title: Eutelsat Retires GEO Satellite 139 West A to Graveyard Orbit
summary: Eutelsat has retired its GEO communications satellite Eutelsat 139 West A after 22 years of service, moving it to a graveyard orbit. The satellite completed its orbit-raising maneuvers before decommissioning.

title: Air India Chief Digital Officer Talks IFC Roadmap at AIX: Video Interview
summary: Air India's Chief Digital and Technology Officer discussed the airline's In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) roadmap at the Aircraft Interiors Expo (AIX) in Hamburg. The interview was conducted by Via Satellite's Senior Editorial Director Mark Holmes.

title: Airline, OEM, Satellite Players Say IFC Experience is in the ‘Details’
summary: Airlines, OEMs, and satellite providers are collaborating to improve in-flight connectivity (IFC) by focusing on customer experience details. Challenges in designing an ideal IFC system were discussed at the Aircraft Interiors (AIX) event.
